after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-rebench-pass31 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/complex_mul_1k_complex128 --sample-size 60 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

benchmark.worker_caveat: Baseline and after used different RCH workers; the delta is large enough to clear the score threshold despite worker variance.

isomorphism_proof.ordering_preserved: Yes. The fast path emits one output per pair in the same zipped row-major tensor order as the generic same-shape loop.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Identical source operation order for Mul: output real is ar*br - ai*bi, output imag is ar*bi + ai*br. No reassociation, fused operation, vector reduction, or approximation is introduced.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Malformed declared-Complex128 tensors still fall through to the generic path by returning None when any element is not Complex128Bits.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: N/A. Complex Mul has no ties or comparisons.
isomorphism_proof.rng: N/A. Complex Mul is deterministic and consumes no random state.
isomorphism_proof.scope: Only same-shape Complex128 tensor Mul changes. Scalar, broadcast, Complex64, Div, Pow, Rem, Max, Min, Atan2, XLogY, LogAddExp, real dtypes, and shape-error paths are unchanged.
